full_prompt:
Design and qualify a flight-critical aluminum 2024-T351 bracket that is classified as a Fracture Critical Part (FCP) per NASA-STD-5019A w/Change-4 (2024-07-29). The bracket holds a 4 kg instrument on a primary load path where failure would cause loss of mission. NASA-STD-5019A Section 4.3 requires the part to be certified by safe-life analysis with a life factor of 4x expected service life (or proof-test logic), and Section 5.2 requires the starting flaw size to be the NDI minimum detectable threshold for the chosen inspection method.

Geometry. Machined 2024-T351 plate bracket 180 mm x 90 mm x 12 mm, featuring four M6 mounting holes on an 150 mm x 60 mm rectangular pattern and a central 25 mm cutout for harness routing. Load introduction is a single M10 pin through a 10.5 mm hole 50 mm from the mounting edge, lateral to the pin axis (bending + shear). Bracket is a single monolithic part.

Material. Al 2024-T351 per MMPDS S-basis: E = 73.1 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2780 kg/m^3, Fty = 324 MPa, Ftu = 441 MPa. Fracture toughness K_IC (through thickness, L-T orientation at 20 C) = 36 MPa*m^0.5. Fatigue crack growth Paris constants (R = 0.1, lab air): C = 2.5e-11 m/cycle (MPa*m^0.5)^-m, m = 3.3.

Service life. Expected operational cycles in orbit: 2000 cycles from launch vibration + deployment + on-orbit dynamic events over 10-year mission. Safe-life factor = 4 per NASA-STD-5019A => required analysis life = 8000 cycles.

Load environment. LC1 (limit): pin-bearing force at limit 3000 N (combined launch + deployment). Ultimate = 1.4 x limit = 4200 N per NASA-STD-5001B. Average cyclic stress range at the critical ligament (between the pin hole and nearest edge): Delta sigma = 120 MPa at R = 0.1 (computed from limit stress cycling up from 10% of limit).

Initial flaw. NDI method: fluorescent penetrant inspection (PT) at Standard NDI reliability per NASA-STD-5019A Table 5-1 => initial crack depth a_i = 0.030 in = 0.762 mm, surface corner crack at the critical ligament edge, shape a_i/c_i = 1.0 (quarter-circle corner crack).

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Safe-life crack growth from a_i = 0.762 mm to critical a_crit (determined by K_I = K_IC) under the cyclic stress history must yield >= 8000 cycles (4 x 2000 service). Use Paris law da/dN = C * (Delta K)^m with Newman-Raju SIF solutions for a corner crack; critical flaw a_crit is reached when K_I = K_IC at peak stress. Numerically for a corner crack at ligament with Delta sigma = 120 MPa, a_crit approx 6.5 mm (a/t = 0.54), and cycles from 0.762 to 6.5 mm under the given Paris law compute to roughly 18,500 cycles (implementation-dependent). Must exceed 8000. (R2) Peak stress at ultimate load = 1.4 x limit = 4200 N bearing, must not exceed Ftu = 441 MPa. (R3) Peak stress at yield load = 1.25 x limit = 3750 N bearing, must not exceed Fty = 324 MPa. (R4) K_I at peak stress and critical flaw depth at ultimate condition must not exceed K_IC = 36 MPa*m^0.5 (residual strength check). (R5) The NDI method declared must meet Standard reliability per NASA-STD-5019A Table 5-1 (binary compliance). (R6) Bracket mass <= 450 g. Fracture Critical classification is self-declared in the deliverables.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary verification is a linear static FEM for peak stress (R2, R3) plus a Paris-law crack-growth integration using Newman-Raju SIF solutions for the corner-crack geometry (R1). A K-solution may be taken from NASGRO or computed via a J-integral FEM extraction. Residual-strength check (R4) is closed-form. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are NOT required; buckling not required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of bracket, mass in g, peak stress at ultimate and yield in MPa, a_crit in mm, cycles-to-failure from integration, and the declared NDI method with its a_i value.
short_prompt:
Design a Fracture Critical (FCP) primary-load-path aluminum bracket holding a 4 kg instrument, qualified by safe-life analysis per NASA-STD-5019A w/Change-4 (2024-07-29). Section 4.3 requires a safe-life factor of 4x expected service life; Section 5.2 requires the initial flaw to equal the NDI minimum-detectable threshold for the chosen method.

Envelope: machined 2024-T351 plate ~180 x 90 x 12 mm with four M6 mounting holes on a 150 x 60 mm rectangular pattern, central harness cutout, and a single M10 pin hole 50 mm from the mounting edge.

Load cases.
- LC1 pin-bearing cyclic service: 3,000 N limit, cyclic Delta sigma = 120 MPa at R = 0.1 in the critical ligament; expected 2,000 cycles, required analytical life 8,000 cycles (4x).
- LC2 ultimate: 1.4 x limit = 4,200 N.
- LC3 yield: 1.25 x limit = 3,750 N.
- LC4 residual strength at peak stress with a crack grown to a_crit, NDI method declared (PT, a_i = 0.762 mm Standard reliability).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: NASA-STD-5019A safe-life is Paris-law + SIF analytic solutions.

prompt.title: Al 2024-T351 Fracture-Critical bracket per NASA-STD-5019A
prompt.brief: Primary-load-path bracket, 4x safe-life (>=8000 cycles from a_i = 0.762 mm PT NDI), K_I < K_IC, ultimate and yield stress within MMPDS allowables.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 4 x 2000 expected service cycles per NASA-STD-5019A Section 4.3 safe-life
K_IC per MMPDS L-T at 20 C

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 for peak stress; SIF analytic for crack

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Cyclic stress history pre-derived as Delta sigma.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Proof-test logic alternative path per NASA-STD-5019A Section 4.3.3
